comments.body: WS1 implemented + committed (Change-Id Idb6de46, local commit). rebar._store/ensures.py (registry, run_ensures under write lock, EnsureOutcome, atomic .ensure-applied via fsutil.atomic_write, absent/garbage→empty). init.py: 5 units refactored to EnsureOutcome units with change-detection; run_ensures wired at re-init + fresh-init + both symlink sites; .ensure-applied added to _GITIGNORE; docstrings updated (init + sync.py). New tests/interfaces/store/test_ensures.py (9 tests, green). make lint + make typecheck green; store interface suite (250 passed) green. Not closing until Gerrit Verified +1 (stacked change).

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Pushed to Gerrit as change 438 (relation-chain base): https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/438 — awaiting LLM-Review + Verified votes.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Init-time `_migrate_*`/`_ensure_*` steps run only at init/re-init, so config fixes never reach
already-existing stores. A first-class idempotent ensure-registry generalizes them (School B
desired-state) so every non-hot entry point converges an existing install.

## What
Create `src/rebar/_store/ensures.py`: a registry of ensure units, each `{stable, immutable id,
callable(tracker: str) -> EnsureOutcome}` where `EnsureOutcome` is a small dataclass
`{id, status: Literal["ok","changed","failed"], detail: str}` and the callable is
check-then-act (no-op when converged). `run_ensures(tracker)` runs ALL units unconditionally
under the STORE write lock (`_store/lock.py` write_lock — init_core does NOT currently hold it;
this is a new dependency), catches a raising unit (skip-and-continue → `failed`), returns the
outcome list, and atomically rewrites `.ensure-applied` to contain ONLY the ids of non-failed
(ok/changed) units via the existing `fsutil.atomic_write` helper (temp-in-SAME-dir + os.replace,
so it is never a cross-device rename; a full rewrite, not an incremental append; absent/garbage/
non-list JSON on read → empty set). Add `.ensure-applied` to `_GITIGNORE`
(init.py:38-48).

`run_ensures()` NEVER raises: besides catching per-unit exceptions, a `write_lock` acquisition
failure (LockTimeout) is caught and treated like a whole-sweep no-op (logged; the caller — init
or MCP boot — continues, never aborts), and a failure to write the `.ensure-applied` marker
itself (OSError, e.g. disk-full/permission) is swallowed best-effort (the sweep's git-level
convergence already happened; the marker is only a hint) — it never fails the sweep or the caller.

Migrate exactly the 5 re-init idempotent units (init.py:209-213): `env-id`, `gc-config`,
`merge-ours driver`, `gitattributes` (COMMIT), `gitignore` (COMMIT). BOTH commit units
tree-check before committing. Wire `run_ensures()` at ALL init entry points and remove the
hand-listed calls: the re-init arm (209-213), the fresh-init arm (eight calls at init.py:241-248:
the migrated units are gitignore(242), gitattributes(245), gc(247), merge(248); the bootstrap
_ensure_branch_user_config(241)/_exclude_scratch_in_tracker(243)/_commit_precommit(244)/
_gen_local_files(246) stay — note `.env-id` is created by _gen_local_files(246) on fresh-init, so
the env-id unit no-ops there and `_ensure_env_id` is NOT among these calls), placing
`run_ensures()` AFTER the fresh-init bootstrap so ordering is preserved.

In `_init_via_symlink`, the two `_ensure_env_id` call sites sit in structurally distinct
branches and each gets its OWN independent `run_ensures(tracker)` call (the function is NOT
restructured to a single unified return): replace `_ensure_env_id(tracker)` at line 564 with
`run_ensures(tracker)` immediately before that branch's `return 0` (the already-symlinked
early-return guard), and replace `_ensure_env_id(tracker)` at line 582 with `run_ensures(tracker)`
immediately before the final `return 0` (the fresh-symlink-creation path); control flow is
otherwise unchanged. Confirm no OTHER callers of the 5 helpers remain after removal (no
double-execution).

The fresh-init-only bootstrap (`_commit_precommit`, `_gen_local_files`,
`_exclude_scratch_in_tracker`, `_ensure_branch_user_config`) is NOT part of the registry and
stays. init_core logs any `failed` units (warning) and NEVER aborts on an ensure failure.
Post-rollback: old code ignores the git-ignored `.ensure-applied` file; committed
`.gitattributes`/`.gitignore` content stays on the tickets branch (harmless/idempotent — old code
tree-checks before committing, so it neither re-commits nor errors on the extra committed content).
Update the stale docstring refs to `_migrate_gc_config` in BOTH `sync.py:17` and the init.py
module docstring (line 8) to point at the ensure registry.

## Scope
In: `ensures.py`, registry + 5 check-then-act units (EnsureOutcome), `run_ensures()` under the
store write lock, `.ensure-applied` format+write + `_GITIGNORE` entry, init wiring at all entry
points (replace + remove old calls incl. symlink-init). Out: pending-hint + `.ensure-hinted`
(WS2), fsck/server wiring (WS3), docs (WS4).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ensures.py` registry: each unit has a stable/immutable id + `callable(tracker) -> EnsureOutcome`; a test asserts the registry's id set equals a frozen expected set (so a rename/typo is caught and applied units can't silently reappear as pending)
- [ ] `run_ensures()` runs all units under the store write lock and returns typed `EnsureOutcome`s; a raising unit is caught (skip-and-continue → `failed`) and excluded from `.ensure-applied`; init logs failures and never aborts
- [ ] `run_ensures()` never propagates an exception to its caller: a `write_lock` acquisition failure (LockTimeout) is caught and logged as a whole-sweep no-op, and an OSError writing `.ensure-applied` is swallowed best-effort; a test asserts init/boot continues (returns 0) when the write lock cannot be acquired
- [ ] All 5 units migrated; BOTH gitattributes and gitignore tree-check before committing; a second `run_ensures()` on a converged store makes zero changes and zero git commits (git-log assertion)
- [ ] Hand-listed calls removed from the re-init arm and the fresh-init arm; in `_init_via_symlink` each of the two `_ensure_env_id` sites (564, 582) is independently replaced by `run_ensures(tracker)` before its own `return 0` (structure otherwise unchanged); no double-execution; no other callers of the 5 helpers remain
- [ ] `.ensure-applied` written atomically via `fsutil.atomic_write` (temp-in-same-dir + os.replace, no cross-device rename) as a JSON array of ONLY non-failed unit ids; it is added to `_GITIGNORE`; absent/garbage/non-list degrades to empty set without crashing
- [ ] An end-to-end test drives `init_core` AND `_init_via_symlink` after the cutover and asserts the store converges (all 5 units applied, `.ensure-applied` written) — not just `run_ensures()` in isolation
- [ ] Stale docstring refs to `_migrate_gc_config` are updated in both `sync.py:17` and the init.py module docstring (line 8)
- [ ] Fresh-init bootstrap steps (_commit_precommit/_gen_local_files/_exclude_scratch/_ensure_branch_user_config) remain and are NOT in the registry

file_impact.reason: wire init_core to run_ensures; remove 5 hand-listed steps

compiled_state.title: WS1: ensure-registry (ensures.py) + migrate 5 check-then-act steps + init wiring
